summary: Runtime enforcement extensions — harness hooks CLI with profile-based activation and 18 new security scanner rules for agent config and MCP auditing

decisions.what: CLI command (harness hooks init) over template-only
decisions.why: Works for both new and existing projects

decisions.what: Add rules to existing SecurityScanner registry, not refactor SecurityAgent
decisions.why: Scanner already has modular registry; review agent serves different purpose (diff analysis)
Avoids coupling to external maintenance cadence

decisions.what: Agent config and MCP as new security categories
decisions.why: Unique to AI-assisted development; not covered by traditional scanners

decisions.what: Security rules are the stronger bet; hooks are nice-to-have at current scope
decisions.why: Agent config auditing is differentiated capability; hooks are convenience over existing permission system
